name: Family Debt Counseling
description_short: Money Management International (MMI) is a nonprofit credit counselor offering free and low-cost debt management, bankruptcy, and housing counseling across the U.S., with HUD and NFCC certification.
description_long:
Money Management International (MMI) is a longstanding nonprofit financial counseling organization founded in 1951 and affiliated with the National Foundation for Credit Counseling (NFCC), the nation's largest nonprofit financial counseling network. The organization holds certifications from the Council on Accreditation (COA), is HUD-certified for housing counseling, and is a member of the Financial Counseling Association of America (FCAA), demonstrating significant institutional credibility and oversight.

MMI offers a comprehensive suite of services including debt management plans, debt resolution plans, bankruptcy pre-filing counseling, homebuyer education, reverse mortgage counseling, and student loan counseling. Clients can access services through multiple channels: in-person appointments in select locations (Phoenix office available Tuesday-Thursday, 9 a.m.–3 p.m.), 24/7 telephone counseling at 866-550-8004, and self-directed online counseling. The organization also provides free educational resources including blog posts, budget guides, podcasts, webinars, and workshops focused on financial literacy.

MMI distinguishes itself through multi-channel accessibility, HUD certification specifically for housing counseling (eviction prevention, homebuyer education), specialty counseling for military families, and disaster recovery counseling. The organization emphasizes a custom-tailored approach, conducting financial reviews and creating individualized savings plans rather than offering one-size-fits-all solutions. All counselors are trained professionals, and services are nonprofit-focused rather than profit-driven.

A key limitation is that MMI's in-person services are available by appointment only and limited to specific hours and select locations. While phone and online counseling are available 24/7, clients seeking immediate face-to-face support may have scheduling constraints. Additionally, while the organization offers debt management and resolution plans, it is fundamentally a counseling and education organization rather than a debt settlement negotiation firm, so outcomes depend heavily on client implementation of recommended plans.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: HUD-certified for housing counseling with specialized eviction prevention and homebuyer education services
24/7 phone counseling availability (866-550-8004) with no geographic restrictions
NFCC member and COA-accredited, indicating independent verification of service quality and ethical standards
Free financial education resources including blogs, podcasts, webinars, workshops, and online homebuyer courses
Specialty counseling for military families, demonstrating targeted support for underserved populations
Multiple service channels: online (self-directed), phone, and in-person counseling to accommodate different preferences
Custom financial reviews and individualized debt management plans rather than standardized approaches
cons: In-person counseling available only by appointment and limited to Tuesday-Thursday, 9 a.m.–3 p.m., creating scheduling barriers for working individuals
No explicit information about whether debt management plans result in actual debt reduction or primarily serve as structured repayment frameworks
As a counseling organization rather than a settlement firm, MMI cannot negotiate directly with creditors on behalf of clients, requiring client initiative
Website does not clearly communicate average costs or whether all services are truly free or require fees for certain plans
Limited detail on success rates or client outcomes for debt management and resolution plans
best_for: Consumers seeking nonprofit credit counseling with verified institutional credibility and HUD/NFCC certification
Homebuyers, renters facing eviction, or those needing housing-related financial counseling with government backing
Individuals preferring 24/7 phone or online counseling over in-person meetings due to schedule or location constraints
Military families seeking specialized financial counseling and education tailored to their unique circumstances
diagnosis: MMI is best suited for consumers who value nonprofit credentials, HUD certification, and 24/7 accessibility over debt settlement speed. Primary caveat: as a counseling organization, MMI educates and guides clients through financial restructuring rather than negotiating debt reductions with creditors, making it ideal for those committed to repayment plans but less effective for those seeking principal reduction through settlement.
services: Free financial education blog posts, guides, and podcasts
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 2333 W Country Gables Dr, Phoenix, AZ 85023
